Biotechnology Research • Lexington, Kentucky • 6,016 followers • 51-200 employees [...] TruDiagnostic is a state of the art epigenetic testing company, specializing in Biological Aging, Nutritional Deficiencies, and Cellular Systems Health based on DNA Methylation. We offer testing for clinical practitioners and their patients, and consumers. TruDiagnostic is dedicated to utilizing validated research and rigorous scientific standards to expand our knowledge and analysis into Epigenetics and Aging. We also participate in research studies, using our validated Biological Age test (TruAge) to verify claims from interventional products. # TruDiagnostic receives CLIA Lab Status and Launches New Portal for Providers and Patients

May 17, 2021- Lexington, KY – Today, TruDiagnosticis proud to announce that their Lexington laboratory has received Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendment (CLIA) in 49 states, including advanced registration in California, Maryland, Rhode Island and Pennsylvania. The company expects to receive the final registration in New York next month. [...] In parallel to its continued lab initiatives, the company has also launched its new dual-facing portal interface, focused around epigenetics and reporting interpretations. Patients and providers alike can now view their results, track data, and receive personalized recommendations based on their own individual epigenetic data, hosted on a HIPAA compliant server to keep personal data safe.

“Created with input from providers and patients alike, this portal is phase one of a continuous development roadmap for our platform”, said Director of Operations, Hannah Went. “We expect to deploy phase two sometime early Q3 with a host of new features.”

These features include:

results.title: Team | TruDiagnostic.com™
results.content:
Dr. Matthew attended medical school at The University of Kentucky before completing his residency in emergency medicine at The University of Utah, where he served as both chief resident and fellow. After practicing medicine and serving as an associate professor at the University of Kentucky for seven years, Dr. Matt developed a deep interest in genomics and epigenetics, realizing their potential to revolutionize our understanding of health and longevity. As the CEO of TruDiagnostic, Dr. Matt leads the company’s mission to advance the science of epigenetics and provide groundbreaking insights into aging and disease prevention. His dedication to optimizing human performance, which began with his personal focus on fitness and nutrition, has evolved into a professional pursuit of harnessing [...] ### Fate/stay night

2014

La città di Fuyuki è il perfetto palcoscenico per lo svolgersi di un antico rituale. Al fine di ottenere il leggendario Santo Graal, sette maghi riceveranno sette Spiriti Eroici scelti dal Graal stesso. Ogni mago stringerà un patto con il proprio Servant al fine di sconfiggere gli altri partecipanti ed ottenere l’agognata ricompensa. Questa è la “Guerra del Santo Graal” e la battaglia inizia ora!

Dr. Pamela W. Smith, MD, MPH, MS [...] Dr. Melissa is a board-certified laboratory geneticist with over 10 years of experience in clinical cytogenetic and molecular genetic analyses, specializing in the diagnoses and management of oncological and germline genetic diseases. She earned her Ph.D. from the University of Kentucky, where she tackled complex genomic issues in evolutionarily significant vertebrate species and served as a graduate-level bioinformatics teaching assistant (including mentoring Dr. Dwaraka, TruDiagnostic’s head of bioinformatics!) Melissa completed a three-year postdoctoral fellowship at the Carnegie Institution for Science, focusing on cytogenetics in lampbrush chromosomes and epigenetic modifications using direct RNA sequencing. During this time, she was a lead teaching assistant for a Practical Genomics

Abstract

36-color spectral flow cytometry (donors aged 20–82) identified CXCR3-negative CD8 memory T cells that accumulate with age, shifting to a Th2-like (type 2) cytokine profile—backed by transcriptomic and epigenetic data—and linked to asthma, chronic liver disease, and type 2 diabetes, tying an age-driven immune shift to multiple chronic conditions.

results.title: Terms of Service | TruDiagnostic.com™
results.content: Your use of the Services (excluding any services provided by TruDiagnostic under a separate agreement) is subject to the terms of the legal agreement between you and TruDiagnostic set forth in these Terms of Service (“TOS“). Except as specified herein, these TOS apply to any use of the Services, including but not limited to a) submitting a blood sample for DNA extraction and processing, b) uploading a digital version of your Epigenetic Information and interacting with it on the TruDiagnostic website, and/or c) creating and using a free TruDiagnostic account without providing your blood sample or Epigenetic Information. In order to use the Services, you must first agree to the TOS. You may not use the Services if you do not accept the TOS. You can accept the TOS by (1) clicking to accept [...] TruDiagnostic Research may study a specific group or population, identify potential areas or targets for therapeutics development, conduct or support the development of drugs, diagnostics or devices to diagnose, predict or treat medical or other health conditions, work with public, private and/or non-profit entities on genetic research initiatives, or otherwise create, commercialize, and apply this new knowledge to improve health care. TruDiagnostic will never release your individual-level Epigenetic Information and/or Self-Reported Information to any third party without asking for and receiving your explicit consent to do so, unless required by law. TruDiagnostic support personnel may temporarily access your account solely for the purpose of diagnosing or resolving a technical issue that [...] Epigenetic Information you share with others could be used against your interests. You should be careful about sharing your Epigenetic Information with others. Currently, very few businesses or insurance companies request epigenetic information, but this could change in the future. While the Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act (“GINA”) was signed into law in the United States in 2008, its protection against discrimination by employers and health insurance companies for employment and coverage issues, respectively, has not been clearly established. In addition, GINA does not cover life, long-term care, or disability insurance providers. Some, but not all, states and other jurisdictions have laws that further protect individuals with regard to their Epigenetic Information. You should

results.content:
#### EPISEEK Multi-Cancer Detection Test

We analyze epigenomic modifications present in the ctDNA. Special software is used to analyze the results and aids the pathologist in issuing a report that specifies if an abnormal signal is detected or if no cancer signal is detected.

EPISEEK detects the presence of the most prevalent lethal solid tumors including:

Lung cancer

Breast cancer

Prostate cancer

Colorectal cancer

Pancreatic cancer

Head and neck cancer

Urinary bladder cancer

Esophageal cancer

And more [...] 20% OFF ALL PRODUCTS + SUBSCRIPTIONS. CODE VDAY26.

SHOP NOW
